items.prompt: Now then — when we're not feeling well, we visit the doctor or nurse. Can you find the nurse?
items.feedback: Nurses take care of people who are poorly — they're very kind and helpful.

items.prompt: Next! The doctor uses this to listen to your heartbeat. It has two earpieces and a round end. Find the stethoscope!
items.feedback: Stethoscope! Lub-dub, lub-dub — that's your heart the doctor can hear.

items.prompt: Now then — this measures if your body is too hot. The doctor puts it in your ear or under your arm. What do you think it is? Find it!
items.feedback: Thermometer! It tells the doctor if you have a temperature.

items.prompt: Your turn! I'm sticky and I cover small cuts to keep them clean. I come in lots of colours. Find the plaster!
items.feedback: Plasters keep cuts clean while your skin heals underneath.

items.prompt: Next up — this is a special box or bag that keeps all the helpful medical things together. Find first aid!
items.feedback: First aid! Wherever there's a first aid kit, help is always nearby.

items.prompt: Now let's find another one! The doctor writes notes about how you're feeling on this. It's a piece of paper on a clipboard. Find the chart!
items.feedback: Chart! The doctor uses it to remember everything about your visit.

items.prompt: Here's another — this symbol looks like a heart. It means staying well and strong. Can you find health?
items.feedback: Health! Eating well, sleeping, and moving around all keep us healthy.

items.prompt: Now! Injections use this to give medicine into your body. It might sting for a second — but it keeps you healthy! Find the needle.
items.feedback: Needle! Brave! Injections help protect us from getting poorly.
